title: Prompt: Extract PDFs
text:
I have a collection of beer recipes as PDFs. I want to extract some specific information from each PDF and save it in a csv file.
Most of the PDFs are structured the same, but I know there are at least some that are different.
Here is the information I want to save. I’m using the recipe “22nd State Amber Ale All Grain Recipe Instructions” as an example:

The recipe name. It’s in a box at the top of the page, under the SoCo Homebrew logo. Like “22nd State Amber Ale”
All the grains listed under “The Mash”. They are in a table after the text “Your kit will contain the following grain:”. You can put all the ingredients in the same field, separated by returns.
In the “Adding the Hops” portion, there are three tables to list hops: for bittering, flavor and aroma. I want those listed as three separate fields. If there is more than one item in one of these tables, keep them in the same field, separated by returns.
I want to record the “original specific gravity”. It is usually a number, like “1.051”
Under pitching your yeast, there is a table that includes different yeasts. For each yeast there is usually a manufacturer like “White Labs” and then the style like “California Ale V 051”. You can put them together like this: “White Labs California Ale V 051”. Include all the yeast varieties in the same cell, separated by returns.
Under “Dry Hopping” I want all the hops listed here in the same cell, similar to others with more than one ingredient.
I want to record the “final specific gravity”. It is usually a number, like “1.012”.
The last thing to record is “Flavoring to add before bottling”. If the table or box says only “No Flavoring”, then leave it blank. Otherwise list the ingredient with returns between multiple ingredients.

Let’s do a single one and check it before doing the other 200+ PDFs.

title: Prompt: Extract PDFs
text:
I have a collection of beer recipes as PDFs. I want to extract some specific information from each PDF and save it in a csv file.
Most of the PDFs are structured the same, but I know there are at least some that are different.
Here is the information I want to save. I’m using the recipe “22nd State Amber Ale All Grain Recipe Instructions” as an example:

The recipe name. It’s in a box at the top of the page, under the SoCo Homebrew logo. Like “22nd State Amber Ale”
All the grains listed under “The Mash”. They are in a table after the text “Your kit will contain the following grain:”. You can put all the ingredients in the same field, separated by returns.
In the “Adding the Hops” portion, there are three tables to list hops: for bittering, flavor and aroma. I want those listed as three separate fields. If there is more than one item in one of these tables, keep them in the same field, separated by returns.
I want to record the “original specific gravity”. It is usually a number, like “1.051”
Under pitching your yeast, there is a table that includes different yeasts. For each yeast there is usually a manufacturer like “White Labs” and then the style like “California Ale V 051”. You can put them together like this: “White Labs California Ale V 051”. Include all the yeast varieties in the same cell, separated by returns.
Under “Dry Hopping” I want all the hops listed here in the same cell, similar to others with more than one ingredient.
I want to record the “final specific gravity”. It is usually a number, like “1.012”.
The last thing to record is “Flavoring to add before bottling”. If the table or box says only “No Flavoring”, then leave it blank. Otherwise list the ingredient with returns between multiple ingredients.

Let’s do a single one and check it before doing the other 200+ PDFs.

title: Prompt: Extract PDFs
text:
Followup prompts
When it finished, it suggested some tests, or to run and look for NAs, or to just go for it. I said: “Test on a few more PDFs — especially ones that look different — before committing to the full batch.”
At this point I was about out of my free credits, so I just wanted a good place to stop so I could do some manual work: “You found there were two different layouts for the PDFs and did a fairly good job of parsing both. The most common one is great, but I need to hand-check the alternative layout. Can you make me a list of the recipes that have the alternative layouts so I can check them?”
It found them and then saved as a csv so I could refer to it.

title: Prompt: Scrape
text: I want to scrape all of the all grain recipes from SoCo Homebrew. I’ll walk you through how I want to approach this.

title: Prompt: Scrape
section: Get the urls for individual recipe order pages
text:
Get the urls for individual recipe order pages
This is the URL that stats listing all the recipe pages:
All Grain Recipe Kits
On that page there are some images that link to groups of recipes. I don’t want those pages. But after the dropdown that says “Featured Items” there starts a list of each recipe’s order page. We’ll want to collect the URL for each of those pages.
At the bottom of the page is a link to advance to the next page, with a URL like this: Page 2. There are 15 such pages. We want the URLs for recipe listed on all 15 pages.

title: Prompt: Scrape
section: Find and download the recipe PDF
text:
Find and download the recipe PDF
Here is an example url for the recipe order page.
One that page is a text leadin “Recipe Sheet Download” with hyperlinked text right after it. That text changes for each recipe, but it links to the PDF for that individual recipe.
For each recipe order page, I want that PDF downloaded into a folder called recipe-pdfs.
I want you to wait 2 seconds between each download.
Build the scraper using the rvest package in the page called 01-scrape.qmd

title: All Grain Beer Recipes
text:
This is a work in progress to create a website to preserve SoCo Homebrew All Grain recipes before the store closes.
The recipes are saved as PDFs.
There are several organizations of the same recipes:

title: All Grain Beer Recipes
text:
Future work?

I’d like the scrape the main information from the pdfs and store in a CSV.

UPDATE: This is mostly done, except I didn’t capture “THE BOIL” portion that tracks extract additions.

I want to make simple Quarto page for each recipe with good indexes that also include links to the original PDFs.
And/or make a database.

title: All Grain Beer Recipes
text:
How this was done

I used Posit Assistant (an AI tool by posit built into RStudio) to build the scraper and pull all the pdfs. See the original prompt in Prompt: Scrape. This yielded the file x-scrape (it was renamed later) that has the code that did the scraping. It took me about 5 minutes to write the prompt. It took working with PA another 15 minutes to get a test version of the scraper, and then 10 minutes for it to download them. (I didn’t save that full conversation with Posit Assistant).
I used PA again to make most of the indexes. You can see the prompts in Prompt: Indexes. I also saved the full conversation here and here in the pa-conversations folder. That also has some followup prompts to fix some titles.
I used PA to extract some key information from the PDFs to put into a csv. Prompt: Extract PDFs. It got most of the way there before I ran out of credits, so I did some cleanup in the file data-processed/receipes.xlsx. Conversation here.

title: Prompt: Indexes
text:
This is the first prompt to build the index of PDFs:
“OK, within the page 02-site-pdfs I want you to create a list in alphabetical order of all the recipes in the recipe-pdfs folder, and link to that recipe. The idea is to when this site is rendered a user could use that page to get to each recipe.”
It suggested some changes to add a search/filter for the page, and to build A-Z breadcrumbs. I said yes to both.
The result was a complicated HTML page, which was fine, but might be harder to maintain, so I suggested this: “While this is fine, can you make a new page that had the original list as a simple Quarto file?”
Then I wanted a different version: “How about this … try to make another recipe list page that organizes the lists by common beer styles. Just use simple Quarto/Markdown headers and not html/javascript.”
Next: “On 02-recipe-list, add Markdown headers for letters of the alphabet.”
Next: “Let’s try another new page: Make another alpha list like in 03-recipe, but if the the recipe name starts with”SoCo”, take that out and re-alphabatize. (Don’t break the link, though!)”

title: Prompt: Indexes
text:
This is the first prompt to build the index of PDFs:
“OK, within the page 02-site-pdfs I want you to create a list in alphabetical order of all the recipes in the recipe-pdfs folder, and link to that recipe. The idea is to when this site is rendered a user could use that page to get to each recipe.”
It suggested some changes to add a search/filter for the page, and to build A-Z breadcrumbs. I said yes to both.
The result was a complicated HTML page, which was fine, but might be harder to maintain, so I suggested this: “While this is fine, can you make a new page that had the original list as a simple Quarto file?”
Then I wanted a different version: “How about this … try to make another recipe list page that organizes the lists by common beer styles. Just use simple Quarto/Markdown headers and not html/javascript.”
Next: “On 02-recipe-list, add Markdown headers for letters of the alphabet.”
Next: “Let’s try another new page: Make another alpha list like in 03-recipe, but if the the recipe name starts with”SoCo”, take that out and re-alphabatize. (Don’t break the link, though!)”

title: Prompt: Indexes
text:
Fix some titles
Next I wanted to clean some stuff up:
“OK … first, I renamed the files, just so you know. There are now four pages that all have”recipes” in the name of the quarto files. What I want you to do next is in the text of each recipe title, take out the phrase ” All Grain Recipe Instructions”. I just want the name of the beer. Make sure not to break the links to the actual filenames!”
Next: “Oh, it looks like there were some names with this phrase that we need to remove:” All Grain Recipe Sheet””
Next: “Also remove”All Grain Brewing Instructions” and “-All-Grain-Recipe” and “All Grain Recipe V2””
